contentDescriptions.description: Classify organisms into major groups based on observable and measurable physical characteristics, and compare their features.
Investigate and describe the cell as the basic structural unit of living things, and identify components that have specialised functions.
Explain how multicellular organisms are organised with specialised cells, tissues, organs and organ systems that work together to enable the organism to survive and reproduce.
Investigate and model the atomic structure of elements, and explain how the arrangement of atoms in the periodic table reflects the properties of elements.
Investigate and describe how substances can be classified as elements, compounds or mixtures, and describe how pure substances can be separated from mixtures.
Investigate how predictable phenomena on Earth, including the seasons and tides, are caused by the relative positions of the Sun, Earth and Moon.
Explain how rocks, minerals and soils are formed and how rock records can be used to reconstruct geological history.
Investigate and describe how forces affect the motion of objects, and explain how balanced and unbalanced forces determine whether an object is stationary or in motion.
Investigate how energy can be transferred and transformed in electrical circuits, and describe the relationship between voltage, current and resistance.
Investigate and explain how the digestive, circulatory and excretory systems work together to maintain the health of the human body.
Describe the role of ecosystems in supporting living things, and explain the impact of human activities on Australian ecosystems.
Investigate and describe the properties of common acids and bases and explain what happens during acid-base reactions.
Classify and describe types of chemical reactions including combustion, corrosion and neutralisation.
Explain how the movement of tectonic plates causes earthquakes, volcanoes and the creation of new landforms.
Describe the evidence that supports the theory of continental drift and plate tectonics.
Investigate and describe how light behaves when it interacts with different materials, including reflection, refraction, transmission and absorption.
Explain how sound is produced and transmitted as a mechanical wave, and describe the factors that affect the properties of sound.
Investigate and explain how the theory of evolution by natural selection explains the diversity of life on Earth, and evaluate the evidence for evolution.
Describe how DNA carries genetic information and explain how genetic information is transmitted from parents to offspring.
Investigate and explain how chemical reactions involve the rearrangement of atoms, and that matter is conserved in chemical reactions.
Describe the properties of metals and explain how they are used in a range of applications based on their properties.
Investigate and explain the evidence for climate change including the role of greenhouse gases, and evaluate the potential impacts on Australian and global environments.
Describe how human activities affect the distribution and availability of natural resources including water, minerals and fossil fuels in Australia.
Analyse data and information about Australia's changing climate and explain the role of human activity in driving observed changes.
Investigate and explain the relationship between the transfer of energy and the movement of particles in thermal and electrical processes.
Explain how energy transformations occur in household electrical devices, and calculate the efficiency of energy transformations.
Investigate and explain how the cell cycle and cell division underpin growth and reproduction in organisms.
Describe the applications and implications of genetic technologies, including selective breeding and genetic engineering.
Investigate and describe the properties of radioactive substances and explain the applications and dangers of radioactive decay.
Explain how atomic theory has developed over time, and describe current models of atomic structure.
Explain how the universe formed and describe current scientific theories about the formation of stars, solar systems and galaxies.
Evaluate the evidence for climate change and compare proposed solutions, considering the social, economic and environmental implications for Australia.
Investigate and explain the relationship between electric fields, magnetic fields and electromagnetic radiation, including the electromagnetic spectrum.
Apply Newton's laws of motion to explain forces and motion in everyday contexts.
Apply advanced scientific inquiry skills to investigate complex phenomena and communicate findings using scientific conventions.
Evaluate and synthesise scientific evidence from multiple sources to construct well-reasoned explanations of scientific phenomena.
Conduct extended scientific investigations and present findings in a format appropriate for professional scientific communication.
Critically evaluate the role of science in addressing major challenges facing Australia, including climate change, biodiversity loss and sustainable resource use.
